council_result: R1 COMPLETE (Grok 4.20 + Gemini 2.5-pro). F1 ADOPT+4 fixes (per-tick target invalidation, staggered think phase, cursor easing, reject logging). F2 OVERRULED-WITH-EVIDENCE -> 6 bots/7 seats (both REJECTs rested on hallucinated structures; the one real consumer is the ui.ts leaderboard pool, one-line fix; 7th color silver 0xc0c8d0; MAX_PLAYERS untouched for wire/lobby; 7-seat invariant test added). F3 ADOPT (Groks 10Hz-quantized-bots rejection architecturally false — human IS the host; isNetworked null-guard audit executed pre-lock, all 11 sites safe). F4 ADOPT+5 fixes incl. CONCEDED->GEMINI PROTOCOL_VERSION 7->8 (stale v7 peer in quickmatch can never send LOBBY_READY = permanent stall), jittered self-promotion, smallest-code convergence, full:true announcements, roster-derived readiness. Full ledger in PDR.

priorities.label: S87 P2 — Bot framework: code-split src/bots/ (config table, controller FSM, manager), virtual-cursor actuation through dispatch, main.ts host-tick wiring
priorities.objective: Bots cruise their avatar (UPDATE_AVATAR_POS <= speed px/tick), claim sparks on arrival (PICKUP_SPARK), haul via S45 coupling, place via PLACE_PRIMITIVE remote-origin path; deterministic seeded RNG; all gates (bench/poop/reach/territory) apply by construction.
priorities.scope: new src/bots/botConfig.ts + botController.ts + botManager.ts (lazy chunk), main.ts tick wiring + teardown
priorities.testing: controller FSM unit tests, determinism same-seed test, bench-gate compliance test, bundle re-measure (index chunk delta ~0)
priorities.check_method: S87 P2 (139ea4b): bot framework verified by integration tests through the REAL dispatch pipeline (no mocks): IMBA bot on a seeded world cruises (avatar displacement >50px from spawn), claims, hauls and places >=3 prims of which >=2 BONDED within 30 sim-seconds; per-tick avatar step bounded <=8px over 600 ticks (NOOB speed 3.2 + wobble 2.5 — no teleports); same-seed double-run produces byte-identical prim/avatar/bond/spark JSON (determinism); benched bot freezes (avatarPos toEqual frozen copy, zero new prims, actorBenched bucket stays 0 = controller parks instead of spamming denied intents) then resumes building after release; NOOB builds strictly fewer prims than IMBA on the same seed+layout. Bundle: ALL bot code code-split (botManager 10.69 kB + botSetupOverlay 4.40 kB lazy chunks); EAGER overlay build measured 566.9 vite-kB = 553.6 KiB = CHARTER BREACH -> lazy split; final index 562.77 vite-kB = 549.58 KiB < 550 KiB. BASELINE UNIT DISCOVERY: S86 "548.3 KiB" = 561.45 vite-kB (worktree-rebuilt 9b3fc4c to prove it) — prior sessions recorded KiB, vite prints kB; both units now quoted. Carry-1 crash class (PICKUP while Carrying throws CarryViolation on un-try/caught dispatch) made unreachable: brain never proposes BUILD while Carrying + controller self-heals idle-with-spark into HAUL + belt-and-suspenders kind check at claim.

priorities.label: S87 P3 — Bot brain: full gameplay (build loop, sever, hazard responses, rainbow/potato/shrink per difficulty)
priorities.objective: Goal arbitration per think-tick: flee hunter / clean splat / claim rainbow / sever (cruise-first) / IMBA potato+shrink / BUILD chain in home sector with difficulty-tuned aim error, cooldowns, bond-density optimization.

priorities.check_method: S87 P3 (281ddb5): brain behaviors proven through the REAL dispatch pipeline (botGameplay.test.ts, 6 tests, all green first run): charged IMBA severs an enemy bond + charge consumed to 0; uncharged bot leaves bonds untouched over 10 sim-seconds (charge economy binds); IMBA triggers the rainbow (rainbows empty + player colors deranged vs before-join string); HARD flees a static hunter >200px while NOOB stays <200px (same seed); IMBA delivers a FREE potato ARMED within 300px of the enemy prim through PICKUP_POTATO/PLACE_POTATO; smart placement yields world.bonds.size strictly > prims-1 (more than a tree = redundancy weaving ran) via componentOf + pickRedundantBondTargets with the HUMAN path constants (k=3, 25deg, AUTO_BOND_RADIUS). Gates UNPIPED: tsc exit 0, full vitest 1349/1349 exit 0, bundle index 562.84 vite-kB = 549.65 KiB < 550 KiB charter with bot chunks lazy (botManager 11.01 kB, botSetupOverlay 4.40 kB).

LIVE PREVIEW (real dev server, screenshots): (1) title shows Solo/Multiplayer/VS Bots/Codex; (2) VS Bots opens the lazy BotSetupOverlay (count stepper + per-bot NOOB/MID/HARD/IMBA + START); (3) started a 3-bot match — all 3 bots cruised, claimed, hauled and built FULLY-BONDED structures and scored income (seat1=12/12, seat2=5/5, seat3=9/9 bonded; leaderboard B2/B3/B4 climbing, P1 human=0), with the human seat 0 at 0 prims/0 score under no input — NO mis-attribution (clean-room re-test confirmed: byOwner={1:1} with zero human prims; the earlier seat-0 prims were an artifact of manual preview poking + harness pointer events, proven by reload+observe); (4) Multiplayer lobby renders QUICK MATCH headline above the unchanged Host/Join panes; (5) zero console errors. E2E: smoke + lobby-construction + nplayer + reconnect run locally — found+fixed 3 expected ripples from the version bump/7th-color (protocol-mismatch v8->current so test moved to v9; v2-test local-version string v7->v8; nplayer 6-player palette assertion sliced to the first MAX_PLAYERS=6) — all re-ran GREEN; remaining hazard specs (bomb/hunter/potato/rainbow/seagull/fog) grep-clean of palette/protocol/mode assumptions, gated by CI. ROADMAP: S87 session entry written; Tier-1 G1a+G3a remains the next build session.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #canary-tests-pay-rent #unit-conventions-bite: the S63 palette canary fired EXACTLY as designed when the 7th seat color landed — it pointed at the duplicated e2e literal that had to move in the same commit. Separately the bundle charter was nearly mis-enforced twice in one session because prior sessions recorded KiB while vite prints kB (548.3 KiB == 561.45 kB): ALWAYS name the unit when recording a gate number, and when a gate reads >2% off baseline, rebuild the BASELINE commit in a worktree before assuming regression.

P2 #bots-are-remote-players #choke-point-dividends: implementing bots as ordinary seated players that may only dispatch CLIENT_INTENT-shaped actions meant every S84-S86 exploit fix (bench gate, poop arrival gates, reach validation, territory blocks) bound the bots ON DAY ONE with zero bot-specific enforcement code — the S86 dispatch-entry choke point paid its first structural dividend. The one crash class was the OPPOSITE direction: reducers that THROW on caller bugs (fsmPickup CarryViolation) are lethal to an autonomous actor, so the controller needs claim-outcome confirmation + self-heal, mirroring what controls.ts does for humans.

P3 #reuse-the-human-path-helpers: the bot redundancy weaving is the human LMB-up path verbatim (componentOf + pickRedundantBondTargets + same constants) — when an AI actor needs a capability a human has, importing the human path pure helpers is both cheaper AND provably consistent (same geometry, same caps), vs re-deriving bot-flavored variants that drift.

P4 #split-the-eager-from-the-lazy-at-the-import-boundary: a new feature whose UI is eager (the lobby quickmatch buttons) breached the bundle charter by ~4 KiB. The fix was NOT to trim the feature but to find pre-existing eager weight that should have been lazy all along — CodexOverlay was eager ONLY because the always-eager godlyOrchestration imported a tiny localStorage helper (unlockGodly) from the same module as the heavy Pixi class. Extracting the helper to codexStore.ts let the 12 kB overlay become a lazy chunk, recovering 3x the headroom needed. LESSON: when an eager addition breaches charter, audit the import graph for heavy modules pulled eager by a TINY shared symbol — splitting that symbol is cheaper + cleaner than shrinking the new feature. Also: the Trystero-importing discovery had to be split from the ready-gate helpers (quickmatch.ts vs quickmatchGate.ts) so hostHandlers could use the gate eagerly without dragging Trystero into index.

P5 #a-version-bump-ripples-into-every-hardcoded-literal: PROTOCOL_VERSION 7->8 + a 7th PLAYER_COLOR broke 5 tests across unit (2 protocol pins, 1 palette canary, 1 netHardening hello-builder) AND e2e (2 protocol-mismatch version literals, 1 nplayer 6-color assertion) — none caught by tsc, all by running the suites. The palette canary FIRED EXACTLY as the S63 author intended (it exists to force the e2e literal to move in lockstep). LESSON: a wire-version or palette-cardinality change is a cross-cutting edit — grep ALL test files (unit AND e2e) for the old literal in the same commit, and expect the e2e lane (not just vitest) to surface the rest. The e2e protocol-mismatch tests specifically encode current/older/newer RELATIVE to PROTOCOL_VERSION, so a bump always moves the newer-peer literal to PROTOCOL_VERSION+1.

P5 #verify-attribution-in-vivo-not-just-in-vitro #harness-pointer-artifacts: the live preview briefly showed the human seat (0) accumulating prims it never built — alarming (the S84 non-builder-win class). Root cause was NOT a bug: synthesized button-clicks + a cursor parked in the play area + the preview harness generating pointer events drove real human builds. A clean-room reload (start match, then ONLY read-only evals, cursor left off-board) proved byOwner={1:1} with the human at 0 — bots attribute to their own seats, the human builds only on real input. LESSON: when a live-preview anomaly looks like a known bug class, do a clean-room re-test that eliminates harness-generated input BEFORE concluding it is real; manual preview pokes (injected entities, synthesized clicks, parked cursor) contaminate attribution.
